reviews.review_description:
This is a well-shot and immersive experience that takes you right on the beach where groups of komodo dragons can be found, into the water to swim with groups of manta rays, and to the highest peaks of the islands to see the beauty of the land. The video is balanced between documentary style nature footage and footage taken that shows the human impact on the environment.
Personally, I wanted more time viewing the dragons, and the above water footage seems noticeably higher-res, so I am surprised so much of this video app was spent on the Manta portion. 
That being said, they are all beautiful creatures, and I know there are heavy safety restrictions while being on Rinta Island filming the dragons. A great effort that I would recommend to anyone for the price A++

I really enjoyed this 20 minute video app.  It starts out showing the beauty of the island and the water that surrounds it, then turns to show the ugliness of the water and land because of the horrible human garage that is destroying the planet.   

It's beautiful and ugly and a lesson the world needs to learn before we kill the planet.   Very educational and great for teenagers to learn and hopefully change the future.
